description: Fix inaccuracies in the architecture and MCP server reference pages: incorrect rate limiting claims, wrong discovery pipeline phase count, incorrect health response types, missing WebSocket messages, and wrong timestamp format.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix rate limiting and /health auth in architecture.mdx
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the architecture docs to accurately describe which endpoints have rate limiting and authentication so that I understand the server's security model.
acceptanceCriteria: The admin endpoints description states that only `/reload` and `/extension/reload` have rate limiting (10 requests/minute), not `/ws-info` or `/audit`
The `/health` endpoint auth is described as 'Optional Bearer token' (not 'None'), with a note that the response includes additional details when authenticated
The endpoint table shows correct auth requirements for each endpoint
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Edit `docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx`.

1. Around line 367: The text says '**Admin endpoints** (`/reload`, `/extension/reload`, `/ws-info`, `/audit`): Bearer token required, plus rate limiting (10 requests/minute)'. Fix to: only `/reload` and `/extension/reload` have rate limiting. `/ws-info` and `/audit` require Bearer token but have no rate limiting. See `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts` — only lines 352 and 373 call `checkEndpointRateLimit`.
2. Around line 50: The endpoint table shows `/health` with Auth 'None'. Change to 'Optional Bearer token'. The health endpoint returns a basic response without auth and an extended response with auth (see `http-routes.ts` lines 246-262).

userStories.title: Fix discovery pipeline phase count in architecture.mdx
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the discovery pipeline description to match the actual code so that I understand the full discovery flow.
acceptanceCriteria: The discovery pipeline is described as five phases (not four): resolve npm, resolve local, load all, merge (local overrides npm), build immutable registry
The merge phase (local plugins override npm plugins of the same name) is documented as a distinct step
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Edit `docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx`. Around lines 77-83, the text says 'four-phase pipeline' and lists: 1. Resolve 2. Load 3. Determine trust tier 4. Build registry. Fix to match the actual code in `platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.ts` line 44 which documents five phases:
1. Discover npm plugins (scan global node_modules)
2. Resolve local plugins (from config.json localPlugins)
3. Load all resolved plugins (package.json, IIFE, tools.json, determine trust tier)
4. Merge (local plugins override npm plugins of the same name)
5. Build immutable registry

The key missing phase is 'merge' — the step where local plugins override npm plugins with the same name.

userStories.title: Fix health response types and WebSocket messages in architecture.mdx and mcp-server.mdx
userStories.description: As a developer integrating with the MCP server, I want the health response schema and WebSocket protocol to be accurately documented so that I parse responses correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: In both architecture.mdx and mcp-server.mdx, `fileWatcher.watchedPlugins` is documented as a number (count), not an array
In both architecture.mdx and mcp-server.mdx, `fileWatcher.pendingPlugins` is documented as a number (count), not an array
In mcp-server.mdx, `lastReloadTimestamp` is documented as a number (milliseconds since epoch), not an ISO 8601 string, with 0 meaning never reloaded
In mcp-server.mdx, the `/ws-info` endpoint does not claim rate limiting

In both architecture.mdx and mcp-server.mdx, the Extension-to-Server WebSocket messages table includes: `plugin.search`, `plugin.install`, `plugin.updateFromRegistry`, `plugin.remove`, `plugin.checkUpdates`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Edit two files: `docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx` and `docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx`.

**Health response fixes:**
1. architecture.mdx ~line 201: Change `"watchedPlugins": ["slack"]` to `"watchedPlugins": 1` and `"pendingPlugins": []` to `"pendingPlugins": 0`. See `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts` lines 282-284, 312-316.
2. mcp-server.mdx ~line 173: Same change — watchedPlugins and pendingPlugins are numbers not arrays.
3. mcp-server.mdx ~line 168: Change `"lastReloadTimestamp": "2026-02-21T10:15:00.000Z"` to `"lastReloadTimestamp": 1740134100000` (a number). Also fix the field description (~line 197) from 'ISO 8601 timestamp' to 'Milliseconds since epoch (0 if never reloaded)'.
4. mcp-server.mdx ~line 208: Remove the rate limiting claim from `/ws-info`. It requires Bearer auth but has no rate limit.

**WebSocket messages:**
5. In both files, find the Extension-to-Server messages table and add these five plugin management messages:
   - `plugin.search` — searches npm registry for plugins
   - `plugin.install` — installs a plugin from npm
   - `plugin.updateFromRegistry` — updates a plugin to latest version
   - `plugin.remove` — removes an installed plugin
   - `plugin.checkUpdates` — checks for available plugin updates

See `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts` lines 404-427 for the actual message handlers.
